launcher.release_notes:
cx-labs-launcher: bump to 0.8.1 — bundle the DoD verify CLI into the .app (#104)

Fixes the "Check" button on the shipped 0.8.0 .dmg: it was calling
locate_cli() which fell back to `go run` in a sibling monorepo checkout
that only exists on my dev laptop. End users saw:

    cx-cs-training-apps not found. Install it with `go install …`,
    or set CX_TRAINING_APPS_BIN to an absolute path.

The Go CLI is now cross-compiled for macOS ARM64 in the release
workflow (before `tauri build`) and dropped into
src-tauri/bundled/cx-cs-training-apps. Tauri picks it up as a
bundle.resources entry and copies it into
Contents/Resources/cx-cs-training-apps inside the shipped .app.
At runtime locate_cli() now resolves it via BaseDirectory::Resource
first and chmod +x's it before spawn (Tauri doesn't guarantee the
exec bit through the copy). Existing dev fallbacks
(CX_TRAINING_APPS_BIN → PATH → sibling `go run`) are preserved
for local iteration.

Also bundles three v0.2-era UI-copy cleanups the user flagged
(Trainings subtitle, sidebar footer note, Settings subtitle) so the
next .dmg users install stops describing the launcher as pre-pod-
deploy MVP with a "v0.3" future.

Workflow (release yaml):
  - actions/setup-go@v5 (also Node-24-native, per the earlier bump)
  - Cross-compile step: GOOS=darwin GOARCH=arm64 go build with
    -trimpath -ldflags "-s -w" for a smaller signed binary

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
